title: Poa jujuyensis
intro: Poa jujuyensis, also known as Jujuy blue grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the Andean region of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Poa jujuyensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Poa jujuyensis include infusions, teas, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Poa jujuyensis is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a type of tussock grass native to South America. The classification of Poa jujuyensis is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Liliopsida, the order Poales, and the family Poaceae. Within the family Poaceae, it is part of the subfamily Pooideae, the tribe Poeae, and the genus Poa. Poa jujuyensis is also known as the Jujuy bluegrass or the Jujuy fescue.
